name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa001
description: A high severity vulnerability CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) was detected in container image.
finding.next_steps: Review and update the container image to patch CVE-2021-44228. Rebuild with a patched base image.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa002
description: High severity vulnerability CVE-2021-45046 found in container image.
finding.next_steps: Update the base image and rebuild the container.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa003
description: VPC Flow Logs are disabled for subnet prod-subnet-1. This reduces network visibility and may violate compliance requirements.
finding.next_steps: Enable VPC Flow Logs on the subnet via Console or: gcloud compute networks subnets update prod-subnet-1 --enable-flow-logs --region=us-central1

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa004
description: VPC Flow Logs are disabled for subnet prod-subnet-2.
finding.next_steps: Enable VPC Flow Logs on the subnet.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa005
description: Private Google Access is disabled for this subnet. Resources without external IP addresses cannot reach Google APIs.
finding.next_steps: Enable Private Google Access for the subnet.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa006
description: Private Google Access is disabled for this subnet.
finding.next_steps: Enable Private Google Access for the subnet.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa007
description: Firewall rule allow-ssh-all has logging disabled. Enable logging to capture connection attempts for security monitoring.
finding.next_steps: Enable logging for the firewall rule.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa008
description: The HTTPS load balancer uses a weak SSL policy that allows TLS 1.0 and deprecated cipher suites.
finding.next_steps: Update the SSL policy to MODERN profile with minimum TLS 1.2. gcloud compute ssl-policies create modern-policy --profile=MODERN --min-tls-version=TLS_1_2

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa009
description: Service account key has not been rotated in over 90 days. Regular key rotation reduces the risk of compromised credentials.
finding.next_steps: Rotate the service account key: gcloud iam service-accounts keys create new-key.json --iam-account=deploy-sa@prod-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa010
description: Access logging is not enabled for Cloud Storage bucket prod-data-bucket. Access logging provides an audit trail for bucket access.
finding.next_steps: Enable access logging: gsutil logging set on -b gs://audit-log-bucket gs://prod-data-bucket

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa011
description: Medium severity vulnerability CVE-2022-32149 found in container image.
finding.next_steps: Update the container image to patch the vulnerability.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa012
description: An anomalous IAM grant was detected. A service account was granted owner permissions.
finding.next_steps: Review and revoke the IAM grant if it was not authorized.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa013
description: Audit logging is not enabled for all services in project prod-project. Audit logs are critical for security monitoring and compliance.
finding.next_steps: Enable audit logging for all services in the project via IAM audit log configuration.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa014
description: High severity vulnerability found in Grafana container image.
finding.next_steps: Update Grafana to the latest patched version.

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa015
description: Firewall rule allow-rdp-all allows unrestricted RDP (port 3389) access from the internet (0.0.0.0/0).
finding.next_steps: Remove or restrict the firewall rule to specific IP ranges: gcloud compute firewall-rules update allow-rdp-all --source-ranges=10.0.0.0/8

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa016
description: Cloud Storage bucket prod-sensitive-bucket has a public ACL that allows allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers read access. This exposes sensitive data to the internet.
finding.next_steps: Remove public IAM bindings: gsutil iam ch -d allUsers gs://prod-sensitive-bucket && gsutil iam ch -d allAuthenticatedUsers gs://prod-sensitive-bucket

name: organizations/123456/sources/1111/findings/aaa017
description: The default Compute Engine service account has been granted the Editor or Owner role at the project level. This violates the principle of least privilege and may allow VM workloads to escalate privileges.
finding.next_steps: Revoke the overly permissive role and create a custom role with only required permissions: gcloud projects remove-iam-policy-binding prod-project --member=serviceAccount:compute-default@prod-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com --role=roles/editor
